name: CareBridge CTAS* RAG Reference Cases
purpose: Small auditable demo index for CTAS* historical-case confirmation in the nurse portal.
importantBoundary: These records are not used to make the final triage decision. Gemma 4 extracts structured facts, the CTAS-aligned rules assign the level, and this index retrieves similar historical cases for nurse-facing confirmation.

sourceDataset.title: Emergency Service - Triage Application
sourceDataset.whyUsableHere: KTAS is a CTAS-derived five-level emergency triage scale. We use it as a public proxy benchmark and historical similarity index, not as official Canadian CTAS training data.

symptoms: Pressure spreading to arm or jaw
cases.summary: Adult chest pressure with arm radiation and breathing concern was treated as emergent.
Loss of consciousness or acute neurologic change was escalated for rapid nurse review.
Breathing difficulty with asthma history aligned with urgent reassessment.
Moderate abdominal pain with higher-risk history was historically triaged as urgent.
Stable isolated limb injury without red flags matched a less-urgent pathway.
Minor skin symptoms without systemic red flags matched non-urgent historical cases.
